title: phanera semibifida var. longebracteata
intro: Phanera semibifida var. longebracteata, also known as the long-bracteate Phanera, is a type of climbing orchid native to Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Phanera semibifida var. longebracteata are attributed to its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and it can also be used in traditional remedies such as poultices and ointments.
